rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; Form Interrogatories, Set One; Demand For Production Of Documents, Set One; And Request For Admissions, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1, Defendant Parker Hannifin Corp.¿s motion to compel responses to special interrogatories, form interrogatories, demand for production of documents and request for admissions is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. Defendant¿s request for monetary sanctions of $1399 is granted. No opposition filed and the facts stated in defendant¿s papers demonstrate plaintiffs¿ abuse of the discovery process. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GLENDA WALLS et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Bryan Steam, Llc To Produce Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Responsive Documents; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2, Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant Bryan Steam, LLC to produce custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and responsive documents is continued to December 11, 2012 per agreement of the parties. If all issues raised by the motion have not been resolved, then by December 6, 2012 at 4:00pm the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, the issues that remain in dispute and then must appear at the hearing on December 11, 2012. = (503/TLJ)
